title: Accounting and Finance Comprehensive Exam
description: A comprehensive collection of 120 MCQs covering financial accounting, bookkeeping, partnerships, and financial statement analysis.

questions.text: H buys goods on credit from J. The goods are unsuitable and H returns them to J. What is the name of the document that H sends to J with the goods?
Why is the general journal used to correct errors in the ledger?
options: To avoid making alterations in the ledger
To provide a record explaining ledger entries

questions.text: Which book of prime entry is part of the double-entry system?
In which of the following will cash discount allowed be first recorded?
A cash account balance was $200. $30 cash was banked and a check for $70 was drawn for cash. What is the final balance?
A bank column shows $1,973 (credit). Unpresented checks are $942 and checks banked are $865. What is the bank statement balance?
Discount allowed column ($842) was posted to Discount Received. Discount received column ($613) was posted to Discount Allowed. How to correct?
The owner of a business takes inventory for her own use. What are the ledger account entries?
A customer's ledger account shows a debit balance of $450. What does this mean?
options: Brown has paid $450 to the business

questions.text: A suspense account was opened with a credit balance of $840. Which error caused this?
options: A check of $420 received was debited to the customer
Goods taken by owner $420 were credited to Drawings

questions.text: A truck cost $8,000, including $36 for petrol. The total $8,000 was debited to the Truck Account. What is the effect?
options: Profit overstated and Assets understated by $36

questions.text: A bookkeeper treats a capital expenditure item as revenue expenditure. What is the effect?
If all machines produce the same revenue, which combination produces the greatest net profit in the first year?
A vehicle costing $20,000 is depreciated by 20% per annum on diminishing balance. What is the depreciation for the second year?
A machine cost $20,000 and is sold for $13,000. Depreciation is 10% on cost. If the provision was $28,000, what is the balance after sale?
What happens to the balance of the Bad Debts Account at the end of a financial year?
options: Transferred to profit and loss section

questions.text: Rent paid: $4,000. Owed on Jan 1: $800. Owed on Dec 31: $1,000. What is the amount charged for rent in the Income & Expenditure account?
Premises rented for $10,000/year from April 1. $10,000 was received by Dec 31. What is the rent amount in the income statement for the year?
A cash discount of $30 was omitted from Smith's account but correctly entered in Discount Allowed. How to correct?
Which item would NOT appear in a sales ledger control account?
What is the primary purpose of control accounts?
Inventory at Dec 2017 was overstated by $6,000. What was the effect on gross profit for the year 2018?
When head office expenses aren't reduced by closure, which action results in highest profit?
A Provision is maintained at 5% of Trade Receivables. How is it shown in the Balance Sheet?
Working capital was $6,000. Receivables paid $1,150 for debts of $1,200. $200 inventory was written off. New working capital?
How is an invoice for an expense owing shown in the Balance Sheet?
How are non-current assets normally recorded in a balance sheet?
If partners have no agreement, how should profits/losses be shared?
Machine cost: $20,000. Life: 5 years. Scrap: $10,000. What is the annual straight-line depreciation percentage?
Net profit: $52,000. Bill gets $12,000 salary. Ratio 3:2. What is credited to partners' Current Accounts?
Which of the following is similar to a club's receipts and payments account?
Opening Capital: $12,000. Closing Capital: $13,800. Net Profit: $3,000. What are the drawings?
What is a limited company's authorized capital?
What is the main reason for preparing a manufacturing account?
Materials: $14,000. Labour: $11,000. Indirect expenses: $4,000. What is the prime cost?
On which basis should a manufacturing company value its finished goods inventory?
Sales: $100,000. Expenses: $30,000. Net profit: $20,000. Gross profit percentage?
Inventory: $80,000. Turnover: 6 times. GP margin: 25% of cost. Forecast sales?
Which profit figures allow proper comparison between a business paying rent and one owning premises?
What is the meaning of the money measurement concept?
Goods bought on credit by X from Y are returned before payment. Where does X record this?
A firm buys goods on credit. Which document does the firm use to record this?
X sends back $800 of faulty goods to Y. In which book of prime entry would Y record this?
List price $1,600. Trade discount 12.5%. Cash discount 3%. Lowest amount paid?
How is a $100 uncredited deposit shown on a bank reconciliation statement?
An amount paid to a supplier was wrongly entered in another supplier's account. Which book corrects this?
A supplier's account in a customer's books has a credit balance of $8,000. This means:
Bob returns goods bought on credit from Tariq. Ledger entries in Tariq's books?
Which error will be discovered by the preparation of a trial balance?
options: Incorrect amount in both Dr and Cr
Incorrect amount entered twice as debit

questions.text: Premises purchased with a 10-year bank loan. Effect on Balance Sheet?
Which of the following is an item of revenue expenditure?
What is the main purpose of charging depreciation?
How is year's net profit affected if reducing balance is used instead of straight-line (assuming higher initial charge)?
A car revalued at the end of Year 2 at $3,800. Which value appears in the Balance Sheet?
Electricity account processing for 2019. (Assume standard accrual logic from prompt context).
Writing off a bad debt not previously provided for. Entries?
Entry in Income Statement for increase/decrease in Provision for Doubtful Debts?
Credit sale to Knight wrongly debited to Smith. Journal entry to correct?
Which item appears on the credit side of a Trade Payables Control account?
Purpose of control accounts?
NCA $180k, Net Current Assets $20k. Acquired for $225k. Goodwill payment?
Closing inventory was undervalued by $1,000. Effect of correcting error?
Bank account balance is $1,200 credit in cash book. How shown in Balance Sheet?
How is working capital calculated?
At what value is inventory stated in the Balance Sheet?
Capital $24,400. Assets (assume NCA) $16,100. Current Liab $4,500. No NCL. Current Assets?
Goodwill from new partner admission retained in books should be:
Sales $128k, Purchases $84k, Op Inv $15.7k, Cl Inv $16.3k, Pur Ret $1.3k. Gross Profit?
Net profit $25,000. Omitted: $1,200 (accrued exp), $400 (prepaid exp). Correct profit?
Partner's current account balance calculation (Assume standard inputs).
Entries for interest on a loan from a partner?
Net profit calculation for a trader from capital changes.
Sales $85,240. GP Margin 20%. Cost of sales?
Buying instruments via cheque for an orchestral society. Where shown?
Auth: 10m shares @ $0.50. Issued: 6m fully paid + 1m @ $0.30 called up. Issued Capital?
What is a direct cost in furniture manufacturing?
Manufacturing costs provided. Prime cost calculation?
Sales $125,000, 25% markup. Overheads $10,000. Net profit percentage of sales?
Which business controls overheads most efficiently (Net Profit / Gross Profit ratio)?
No adjustment for prepayment of insurance. Effect on profit?
Which account usually has a debit balance?
X invoices goods to Y. They are found to be damaged. Which document will X then send to Y?
What is true about cash discounts received?
What is shown in a bank reconciliation statement prepared after the cashbook update?
options: Cheques debited in error by bank

questions.text: Bank statement $2,500. Cashbook $1,750. Unpresented checks $750. Balance Sheet figure?
Which transaction is entered in the general journal?
options: Purchase of a non-current asset on credit

questions.text: Which ledger entries record the purchase of a machine bought on credit?
Sales of $2,800 have been debited to Sales account. Effect on balance?
After which error will a trial balance still balance?
options: Error in adding a receivable account
Error in entering amount on a sales invoice
Error in recording cash in cashbook

questions.text: Purchase returns $150 wrongly posted to debit of Sales Returns. Effect on Trial Balance?
A business receives rent for letting premises. How is this classified?
Machinery cost debited to Purchases and included in closing inventory. Effect on Net Profit?
Vehicle cost $20,000, 20% reducing balance. Sold after 2 years at no profit/loss. Sale price?
Cost $100,000, 25% straight line. NBV at end of year 3?
Cost $8,000, sold for $4,800. NBV $3,000. Profit or loss?
Provision $30, Trade Receivables $360. New provision 5% of receivables. Entry in I.S.?
Electricity accrual of $450 treated as prepayment. Effect on net profit?
Sales journal page overcast by $600. Correcting entry?
Trade receivables balance in Balance Sheet calculation.
X assets $17k, CL $3k. Y buys X assets (valued $19.5k) for $20k and takes over CL. Goodwill?
Closing inventory $4,000 added to Op Inv and Purchases in trading section. Effect on GP?
Sole trader writes off a bad debt. Effect on Balance Sheet?
What is the purpose of preparing a balance sheet?
What is the correct method of valuing inventory?
What is shown in a balance sheet as an asset?
What does current assets less current liabilities equal?
Partner capital balances calculation (Assume goodwill adjustment).
Insurance prepaid $60, wages outstanding $1,560. Net profit calculation.
Profit $5,000. Closing inventory $100 overlooked, rent receivable $150 owing. Correct profit?
Increase provision for doubtful debts by $500. Effect on final accounts?
Partnership residue profit calculation (Net Profit $100k, less interest/salary).
Partner receives interest on capital. Bookkeeping entries?
Subscription calculation for Income and Expenditure account.
Correcting purchases using trade payables account logic.
Net profit calculation from capital and drawings change.
Which formula is used to calculate the rate of inventory turnover?
Sales calculation from margin/GP data.
Gross margin constant, net margin increased. What happened?
Stationery inventories only recorded as asset if over $100. Concept?
